skill.name: quality-manager-qms-iso13485
skill.description: ISO 13485 Quality Management System guidance for medical device companies. It supports QMS design, document control, management review, internal audits, CAPA workflows, supplier quality, and certification readiness.
skill.summary: Guides ISO 13485 QMS planning, documentation, audits, reviews, CAPA, and supplier quality work.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported weak cryptography, shell backtick, and configuration-library patterns. Manual review found these are false positives from ISO 13485 prose, Markdown fences, inline file names, and ordinary compliance terms. No prompt injection, credential access, exfiltration, or executable command behavior was found in the reviewed cited files.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Matches in Documentation Text
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptographic algorithm patterns, but the cited lines contain ISO 13485 descriptions, Markdown headings, asset examples, and reference guide outline text. I did not find evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or any cryptographic operation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are plain documentation content and do not call cryptographic APIs. The pattern appears to be caused by broad text matching rather than executable code.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Treated as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution, but the cited lines are a Markdown code fence and inline file or directory names in a resource list. These backticks are formatting markers, not runtime command execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding context is Markdown prose and resource documentation. No script interpreter, command string, user input, or command execution API is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Environment Access Pattern in Compliance Terms
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged configuration library access, but the cited lines contain normal quality management terms such as nonconformity, corrective action, certificate maintenance, and renewal planning. I did not find evidence of environment variable reads or secret handling.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are compliance workflow bullets, not code. There is no process.env, config loader, credential reference, or network destination.

content.value_statement: Medical device teams need controlled, audit-ready quality processes. This skill helps structure ISO 13485 QMS work, documentation, reviews, audits, CAPA, and supplier quality activities.
actual_capabilities: Outlines ISO 13485 QMS implementation steps and gap analysis activities.
Structures document control lifecycles, approvals, distribution, retention, and disposal.
Prepares management review inputs, action tracking, and follow-up verification.
Plans internal audit programs with scope, criteria, frequency, and follow-up.
Guides design controls, risk management integration, and supplier quality processes.
Defines practical QMS performance indicators for monitoring and improvement.
limitations: It does not replace legal, regulatory, or certification body advice.
It does not verify that company records meet all local regulatory requirements.
It provides guidance, not a complete electronic QMS platform.
Referenced template and script names may require local implementation before use.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Certification Roadmap
use_cases.description: Create a phased ISO 13485 implementation plan with gaps, priorities, owners, milestones, and review points.

use_cases.title: Standardize QMS Documents
use_cases.description: Define document control rules for templates, approvals, distribution, retention, superseded documents, and external records.

use_cases.title: Plan Internal Audits
use_cases.description: Build an annual audit program with risk-based scheduling, audit scope, evidence needs, and CAPA follow-up.

prompt_templates.title: Create a QMS Gap Checklist
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for an ISO 13485 readiness review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an ISO 13485 QMS gap checklist for a small medical device company. Include clauses, evidence to request, risk level, and responsible roles.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Document Control Process
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for a controlled procedure outline.
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft a document control process for ISO 13485 clause 4.2.3. Cover creation, approval, release, distribution, revision, archiving, and disposal.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Management Review Agenda
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for a quarterly leadership review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a quarterly management review agenda for ISO 13485 clause 5.6. Include required inputs, decisions, output records, owners, and follow-up tracking.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Risk-Based Audit Program
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for a full annual internal audit plan.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a risk-based annual internal audit program for ISO 13485. Include process criticality, audit frequency, scope, criteria, auditor competence, and CAPA follow-up.

output_examples.input: Create a document control checklist for ISO 13485.
output: Review and approval evidence recorded before release.
Current revision available at points of use.
Obsolete versions removed or clearly marked.
Retention period and disposal authority defined.

output_examples.input: Plan management review inputs for next quarter.
output: Customer feedback, complaints, and trend signals.
Process performance and product conformity metrics.
Changes that may affect the QMS.
On-time delivery impact on product quality.

best_practices: Provide your product type, regulatory markets, company size, and current QMS maturity before asking for plans.
Ask for outputs that include owners, evidence, records, due dates, and effectiveness checks.
Have qualified regulatory or quality leaders review generated procedures before controlled release.
anti_patterns: Do not treat generated procedures as approved controlled documents without review.
Do not omit local regulatory requirements when adapting ISO 13485 guidance.
Do not use generic audit questions without linking them to process risks and objective evidence.

faq.question: Can this skill create a full ISO 13485 QMS?
faq.answer: It can help structure the work and draft materials. Your organization must validate, approve, implement, and maintain the QMS.

faq.question: Does it provide certification advice?
faq.answer: It provides preparation guidance. Certification decisions and formal interpretations belong to accredited bodies and qualified regulatory professionals.

faq.question: Can it support internal audits?
faq.answer: Yes. It can help plan audit scope, criteria, evidence requests, checklists, findings, and CAPA follow-up.

faq.question: Does it cover design controls?
faq.answer: Yes. It outlines design planning, inputs, outputs, reviews, verification, validation, transfer, and change control.

faq.question: Can it integrate ISO 14971 risk management?
faq.answer: Yes. It can align risk management planning, analysis, controls, verification, and post-production monitoring with QMS processes.

faq.question: Is it suitable for all medical device companies?
faq.answer: It is most useful for teams applying ISO 13485. Local laws, product risk, and market requirements still need expert review.
